*flagDuration)\n140\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Space: %.1fx%.1fx%.1f m\", space.Width, space.Depth, space.Height)\n141\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   BLE: %v\", *flagBLE)\n142\t\n143\t\t// Create context for reject)\n162\t\trunSimulation(ctx, nodes, walkers, space, rng, rejectChan)\n163\t\n164\t\t// Shutdown\n165\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Shutting down...\")\n166\t\tfor _, node := range nodes float64\n187\t\t_, err := fmt.Sscanf(s, \"%fx%fx%f\", &w, &d, &h)\n188\t\tif err != nil {\n189\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid format (expected WxDxH): %w\", err)\n190\t\t}\n191\t\tif w <= 0 || d <= 0 || h <= 0 {\n192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"dimensions must be positive\")\n193\t\t}\n194\t\treturn &Space{Width: w, Depth: d, Height: h}, nil\n195\t}\n196\t\n197\t// createVirtualNodes creates virtual nodes at corners, evenly distributed\n198\tfunc createVirtualNodes(count int, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) []*VirtualNode {\n199\t\tnodes := make([]*VirtualNode, count)\n200\t\n201\t\t// Position nodes at corners and midpoints\n202\t\tpositions := generateNodePositions(count, space)\n203\t\n204\t\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n205\t\t\tmac := generateMAC(i)\n206\t\t\tnodes[i] = &VirtualNode{\n207\t\t\t\tID:       i,\n208\t\t\t\tMAC:      mac,\n209\t\t\t\tPosition: positions[i],\n210\t\t\t}\n211\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: MAC=%s pos=(%.2f,%.2f,%.2f)\",\n212\t\t\t\ti, macToString(mac), positions[i].X, positions[i].Y, positions[i].Z)\n213\t\t}\n214\t\n215\t\treturn nodes\n216\t}\n217\t\n218\t// generateNodePositions generates positions for nodes evenly distributed in the space\n219\tfunc generateNodePositions(count int, space *Space) []Point {\n220\t\tpositions := make([]Point, count)\n221\t\n222\t\t// For small counts, use corners\n223\t\t// For larger counts, distribute evenly\n224\t\tif count == 1 {\n225\t\t\tpositions[0] = Point{X: space.Width / 2, Y: space.Depth / 2, Z: space.Height / 2}\n226\t\t} else if count == 2 {\n227\t\t\tpositions[0] = Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n228\t\t\tpositions[1] = Point{X: space.Width, Y: space.Depth, Z: space.Height}\n229\t\t} else if count == 3 {\n230\t\t\tpositions[0] = Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n231\t\t\tpositions[1] = Point{X: space.Width, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n232\t\t\tpositions[2] = Point{X: space.Width / 2, Y: space.Depth, Z: 0}\n233\t\t} else if count == 4 {\n234\t\t\tpositions[0] = Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n235\t\t\tpositions[1] = Point{X: space.Width, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n236\t\t\tpositions[2] = Point{X: 0, Y: space.Depth, Z: space.Height}\n237\t\t\tpositions[3] = Point{X: space.Width, Y: space.Depth, Z: space.Height}\n238\t\t} else {\n239\t\t\t// For more than 4 nodes, distribute in a grid pattern\n240\t\t\tgridSize := int(math.Ceil(math.Sqrt(float64(count))))\n241\t\t\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n242\t\t\t\trow := i / gridSize\n243\t\t\t\tcol := i % gridSize\n244\t\t\t\tpositions[i] = Point{\n245\t\t\t\t\tX: float64(col) * space.Width / float64(gridSize-1),\n246\t\t\t\t\tY: float64(row) * space.Depth / float64(gridSize-1),\n247\t\t\t\t\tZ: space.Height / 2,\n248\t\t\t\t}\n249\t\t\t}\n250\t\t}\n251\t\n252\t\treturn positions\n253\t}\n254\t\n255\t// generateMAC generates a MAC address for a virtual node\n256\tfunc generateMAC(id int) [6]byte {\n257\t\tvar mac [6]byte\n258\t\t// Use a predictable OUI + node ID\n259\t\tmac[0] = 0x02 // Locally administered\n260\t\tmac[1] = 0x53 // Spaxel OUI (fictional)\n261\t\tmac[2] = 0xAC\n262\t\tmac[3] = byte((id >> 16) & 0xFF)\n263\t\tmac[4] = byte((id >> 8) & 0xFF)\n264\t\tmac[5] = byte(id & 0xFF)\n265\t\treturn mac\n266\t}\n267\t\n268\t// macToString converts a 6-byte MAC to colon-separated hex\n269\tfunc macToString(mac [6]byte) string {\n270\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X\",\n271\t\t\tmac[0], mac[1], mac[2], mac[3], mac[4], mac[5])\n272\t}\n273\t\n274\t// createWalkers creates walkers with random walk behavior\n275\tfunc createWalkers(count int, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) []*Walker {\n276\t\twalkers := make([]*Walker, count)\n277\t\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n278\t\t\twalkers[i] = &Walker{\n279\t\t\t\tID:       i,\n280\t\t\t\tPosition: Point{X: space.Width / 2, Y: space.Depth / 2, Z: 1.0}, // Start in center\n281\t\t\t\tVelocity: Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: 0},\n282\t\t\t}\n283\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Walker %d: starting at (%.2f,%.2f,%.2f)\",\n284\t\t\t\ti, walkers[i].Position.X, walkers[i].Position.Y, walkers[i].Position.Z)\n285\t\t}\n286\t\treturn walkers\n287\t}\n288\t\n289\t// contextWithCancel creates a context that can be cancelled\n290\tfunc contextWithCancel() (context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {\n291\t\treturn context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n292\t}\n293\t\n294\t// connectNodes connects all virtual nodes to the mothership via WebSocket\n295\tfunc connectNodes(ctx context.Context, nodes []*VirtualNode, token string, rng *rand.Rand, rejectChan chan<- struct{}) error {\n296\t\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n297\t\terrChan := make(chan error, len(nodes))\n298\t\n299\t\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n300\t\t\twg.Add(1)\n301\t\t\tgo func(n *VirtualNode) {\n302\t\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n303\t\n304\t\t\t\t// Build WebSocket URL with token in header\n305\t\t\t\tu, err := url.Parse(*flagMothership)\n306\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n307\t\t\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"node %d: invalid URL: %w\", n.ID, err)\n308\t\t\t\t\treturn\n309\t\t\t\t}\n310\t\n311\t\t\t\t// Create request with token header\n312\t\t\t\treqHeader := http.Header{}\n313\t\t\t\treqHeader.Set(\"X-Spaxel-Token\", token)\n314\t\n315\t\t\t\t// Connect to WebSocket\n316\t\t\t\tconn, resp, err := websocket.DefaultDialer.DialContext(ctx, u.String(), reqHeader)\n317\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n318\t\t\t\t\tif resp != nil {\n319\t\t\t\t\t\t// Check for reject response\n320\t\t\t\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode == http.StatusUnauthorized || resp.StatusCode == http.StatusForbidden {\n321\t\t\t\t\t\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n322\t\t\t\t\t\t\tresp.Body.Close()\n323\t\t\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: REJECT response from mothership (status %d): %s\", n.ID, resp.StatusCode, string(body))\n324\t\t\t\t\t\t\tstats.Rejected.Store(true)\n325\t\t\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n326\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcase rejectChan <- struct{}{}:\n327\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n328\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n329\t\t\t\t\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"node %d: rejected by mothership\", n.ID)\n330\t\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n331\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n332\t\t\t\t\t\tresp.Body.Close()\n333\t\t\t\t\t}\n334\t\t\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"node %d: connection failed: %w\", n.ID, err)\n335\t\t\t\t\treturn\n336\t\t\t\t}\n337\t\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n338\t\n339\t\t\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n340\t\t\t\tn.Conn = conn\n341\t\t\t\tn.mu.Unlock()\n342\t\n343\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: connected to mothership\", n.ID)\n344\t\n345\t\t\t\t// Send hello message. Announce the node's computed position\n346\t\t\t\t// (createVirtualNodes corner geometry) so the mothership persists it\n347\t\t\t\t// in the fleet/DB row instead of leaving it at the schema default (bf-24xp).\n348\t\t\t\thello := map[string]interface{}{\n349\t\t\t\t\t\"type\":            \"hello\",\n350\t\t\t\t\t\"mac\":             macToString(n.MAC),\n351\t\t\t\t\t\"firmware_version\": \"sim-1.0.0\",\n352\t\t\t\t\t\"capabilities\":    []string{\"csi\", \"ble\", \"tx\", \"rx\"},\n353\t\t\t\t\t\"chip\":            \"ESP32-S3\",\n354\t\t\t\t\t\"flash_mb\":        16,\n355\t\t\t\t\t\"uptime_ms\":       1000,\n356\t\t\t\t\t\"pos_x\":           n.Position.X,\n357\t\t\t\t\t\"pos_y\":           n.Position.Y,\n358\t\t\t\t\t\"pos_z\":           n.Position.Z,\n359\t\t\t\t}\n360\t\t\t\tif err := conn.WriteJSON(hello); err != nil {\n361\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: failed to send hello: %v\", n.ID, err)\n362\t\t\t\t\terrChan <- err\n363\t\t\t\t\treturn\n364\t\t\t\t}\n365\t\n366\t\t\t\t// Listen for downstream messages (role assignment, config, reject)\n367\t\t\t\tgo n.listenForDownstream(ctx, rejectChan)\n368\t\t\t}(node)\n369\t\t}\n370\t\n371\t\twg.Wait()\n372\t\tclose(errChan)\n373\t\n374\t\t// Check for errors\n375\t\tvar errs []error\n376\t\tfor err := range errChan {\n377\t\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n378\t\t}\n379\t\n380\t\tif len(errs) > 0 {\n381\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"connection errors: %v\", errs)\n382\t\t}\n383\t\n384\t\treturn nil\n385\t}\n386\t\n387\t// listenForDownstream listens for downstream messages from the mothership\n388\tfunc (n *VirtualNode) listenForDownstream(ctx context.Context, rejectChan chan<- struct{}) {\n389\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n390\t\tconn := n.Conn\n391\t\tn.mu.Unlock()\n392\t\n393\t\tdefer func() {\n394\t\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n395\t\t\tif n.Conn == conn {\n396\t\t\t\tn.Conn = nil\n397\t\t\t}\n398\t\t\tn.mu.Unlock()\n399\t\t\tconn.Close()\n400\t\t}()\n401\t\n402\t\tfor {\n403\t\t\tselect {\n404\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n405\t\t\t\treturn\n406\t\t\tdefault:\n407\t\t\t}\n408\t\n409\t\t\tvar msg json.RawMessage\n410\t\t\tif err := conn.ReadJSON(&msg); err != nil {\n411\t\t\t\tif ctx.Err() != nil {\n412\t\t\t\t\treturn\n413\t\t\t\t}\n414\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: read error: %v\", n.ID, err)\n415\t\t\t\treturn\n416\t\t\t}\n417\t\n418\t\t\t// Parse message type\n419\t\t\tvar typeMsg struct {\n420\t\t\t\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n421\t\t\t}\n422\t\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(msg, &typeMsg); err != nil {\n423\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: invalid message: %s\", n.ID, string(msg))\n424\t\t\t\tcontinue\n425\t\t\t}\n426\t\n427\t\t\tswitch typeMsg.Type {\n428\t\t\tcase \"reject\":\n429\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: REJECT message received: %s\", n.ID, string(msg))\n430\t\t\t\tstats.Rejected.Store(true)\n431\t\t\t\tselect {\n432\t\t\t\tcase rejectChan <- struct{}{}:\n433\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n434\t\t\t\t}\n435\t\t\t\treturn\n436\t\t\tcase \"role\", \"config\":\n437\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: received %s message\", n.ID, typeMsg.Type)\n438\t\t\tcase \"ota\", \"reboot\", \"identify\", \"baseline_request\":\n439\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: received %s message (acknowledged)\", n.ID, typeMsg.Type)\n440\t\t\tdefault:\n441\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: received unknown message type: %s\", n.ID, typeMsg.Type)\n442\t\t\t}\n443\t\t}\n444\t}\n445\t\n446\t// runSimulation runs the main simulation loop\n447\tfunc runSimulation(ctx context.Context, nodes []*VirtualNode, walkers []*Walker, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand, rejectChan <-chan struct{}) {\n448\t\tstats.StartTime = time.Now()\n449\t\tstats.LastStatsTime = stats.StartTime\n450\t\n451\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(time.Duration(1000/(*flagRate)) * time.Millisecond)\n452\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n453\t\n454\t\tbleTicker := time.NewTicker(5 * time.Second)\n455\t\tdefer bleTicker.Stop()\n456\t\n457\t\tdurationTimer := time.NewTimer(time.Duration(*flagDuration) * time.Second)\n458\t\tif *flagDuration == 0 {\n459\t\t\tdurationTimer.Stop()\n460\t\t}\n461\t\n462\t\tframeNum := 0\n463\t\twalkerUpdateTicker := time.NewTicker(50 * time.Millisecond) // Update walkers every 50ms\n464\t\tdefer walkerUpdateTicker.Stop()\n465\t\n466\t\t// Handle interrupt signal\n467\t\tsigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)\n468\t\tsignal.Notify(sigChan, os.Interrupt)\n469\t\n470\t\tfor {\n471\t\t\tselect {\n472\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n473\t\t\t\treturn\n474\t\t\tcase <-sigChan:\n475\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Interrupted, shutting down...\")\n476\t\t\t\treturn\n477\t\t\tcase <-durationTimer.C:\n478\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Duration elapsed, shutting down...\")\n479\t\t\t\treturn\n480\t\t\tcase <-rejectChan:\n481\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node rejected by mothership, exiting...\")\n482\t\t\t\tstats.Rejected.Store(true)\n483\t\t\t\treturn\n484\t\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n485\t\t\t\t// Send CSI frames for all TX->RX pairs\n486\t\t\t\tfor _, tx := range nodes {\n487\t\t\t\t\tfor _, rx := range nodes {\n488\t\t\t\t\t\tif tx.ID == rx.ID {\n489\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip self-pairs\n490\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n491\t\n492\t\t\t\t\t\tframe := generateCSIFrame(tx, rx, walkers, frameNum, rng)\n493\t\n494\t\t\t\t\t\ttx.mu.Lock()\n495\t\t\t\t\t\tconn := tx.Conn\n496\t\t\t\t\t\ttx.mu.Unlock()\n497\t\n498\t\t\t\t\t\tif conn != nil {\n499\t\t\t\t\t\t\tif err := conn.WriteMessage(websocket.BinaryMessage, frame); err != nil {\n500\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: send error: %v\", tx.ID, err)\n501\t\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n502\t\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n503\t\t\t\t\t\t\tstats.FramesSent.Add(1)\n504\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n505\t\t\t\t\t}\n506\t\t\t\t}\n507\t\t\t\tframeNum++\n508\t\t\tcase <-walkerUpdateTicker.C:\n509\t\t\t\t// Update walker positions (random walk)\n510\t\t\t\tupdateWalkers(walkers, space, rng)\n511\t\t\tcase <-bleTicker.C:\n512\t\t\t\tif *flagBLE {\n513\t\t\t\t\tsendBLEAdvertisements(nodes, rng)\n514\t\t\t\t}\n515\t\t\t}\n516\t\t}\n517\t}\n518\t\n519\t// generateCSIFrame generates a synthetic CSI binary frame\n520\tfunc generateCSIFrame(tx, rx *VirtualNode, walkers []*Walker, frameNum int, rng *rand.Rand) []byte {\n521\t\t// Calculate combined CSI from all walkers\n522\t\tamplitude, phaseBase := computeCSIForWalkers(tx, rx, walkers)\n523\t\n524\t\t// Compute RSSI from amplitude\n525\t\trssi := amplitudeToRSSI(amplitude)\n526\t\n527\t\t// Create frame buffer\n528\t\tframe := make([]byte, headerSize+nSub*2)\n529\t\n530\t\t// Write header (matches ingestion/frame.go ParseFrame layout)\n531\t\tcopy(frame[0:6], tx.MAC[:])   // node_mac\n532\t\tcopy(frame[6:12], rx.MAC[:])  // peer_mac\n533\t\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(frame[12:20], uint64(frameNum*50000)) // timestamp_us\n534\t\tframe[20] = byte(int8(rssi))  // rssi\n535\t\tframe[21] = byte(-95 & 0xFF)   // noise_floor: -95 dBm\n536\t\tframe[22] = byte(defaultChannel) // channel\n537\t\tframe[23] = nSub              // n_sub\n538\t\n539\t\t// Generate I/Q pairs for each subcarrier\n540\t\tfor k := 0; k < nSub; k++ {\n541\t\t\t// Phase for this subcarrier\n542\t\t\tphase := phaseBase + float64(k)*0.1\n543\t\n544\t\t\t// Add temporal variation\n545\t\t\tphase += 0.1 * math.Sin(2*math.Pi*float64(frameNum)/100.0)\n546\t\n547\t\t\t// Normalize phase to [-π, π]\n548\t\t\tfor phase > math.Pi {\n549\t\t\t\tphase -= 2 * math.Pi\n550\t\t\t}\n551\t\t\tfor phase < -math.Pi {\n552\t\t\t\tphase += 2 * math.Pi\n553\t\t\t}\n554\t\n555\t\t\t// Add frequency-selective fading\n556\t\t\tfreqFading := 0.8 + 0.4*math.Sin(2*math.Pi*float64(k)/16.0)\n557\t\t\tsubAmplitude := amplitude * freqFading\n558\t\n559\t\t\t// Add Gaussian noise\n560\t\t\tamplitudeNoisy := subAmplitude * (1 + randNorm(rng, 0, 0.05))\n561\t\n562\t\t\t// Generate I/Q\n563\t\t\ti, q := generateIQPair(amplitudeNoisy, phase, rng)\n564\t\n565\t\t\t// Write to payload (interleaved I,Q)\n566\t\t\toffset := headerSize + k*2\n567\t\t\tframe[offset] = byte(int8(i))\n568\t\t\tframe[offset+1] = byte(int8(q))\n569\t\t}\n570\t\n571\t\treturn frame\n572\t}\n573\t\n574\t// computeCSIForWalkers computes the combined CSI amplitude and phase from all walkers\n575\tfunc computeCSIForWalkers(tx, rx *VirtualNode, walkers []*Walker) (float64, float64) {\n576\t\tif len(walkers) == 0 {\n577\t\t\t// No walkers, return baseline noise\n578\t\t\treturn 0.001, 0.0\n579\t\t}\n580\t\n581\t\tvar totalAmplitude float64\n582\t\tvar totalPhase float64\n583\t\tvar weight float64\n584\t\n585\t\tfor _, walker := range walkers {\n586\t\t\t// Direct path contribution\n587\t\t\tdirectAmp, directPhase := computeDirectPath(tx.Position, rx.Position, walker.Position)\n588\t\n589\t\t\t// Scale to reasonable values\n590\t\t\tcombinedAmp := directAmp * 1000.0\n591\t\n592\t\t\t// Accumulate\n593\t\t\ttotalAmplitude += combinedAmp\n594\t\t\ttotalPhase += directPhase\n595\t\t\tweight += 1.0\n596\t\t}\n597\t\n598\t\t// Normalize phase\n599\t\tif weight > 0 {\n600\t\t\ttotalPhase /= weight\n601\t\t}\n602\t\n603\t\treturn totalAmplitude, totalPhase\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// computeDirectPath computes the CSI contribution from the direct path\n607\tfunc computeDirectPath(tx, rx, walker Point) (float64, float64) {\n608\t\t// Distance from TX to walker\n609\t\td1 := distance(tx, walker)\n610\t\t// Distance from walker to RX\n611\t\td2 := distance(walker, rx)\n612\t\t// Total path length\n613\t\tdTotal := d1 + d2\n614\t\n615\t\t// Direct TX-RX distance (for Fresnel zone calculation)\n616\t\tdDirect := distance(tx, rx)\n617\t\n618\t\t// Path length excess for Fresnel zone calculation\n619\t\texcess := dTotal - dDirect\n620\t\tif excess < 0 {\n621\t\t\texcess = 0\n622\t\t}\n623\t\n624\t\t// Fresnel zone number\n625\t\tzoneNumber := int(math.Ceil(excess / halfWavelength))\n626\t\tif zoneNumber < 1 {\n627\t\t\tzoneNumber = 1\n628\t\t}\n629\t\n630\t\t// Zone decay (inverse square)\n631\t\tdecay := 1.0 / math.Pow(float64(zoneNumber), 2.0)\n632\t\n633\t\t// Log-distance path loss model: PL(d) = PL_0 + 10*n*log10(d/d_0)\n634\t\tvar pathLossDB float64\n635\t\tif dTotal >= 1.0 {\n636\t\t\tpathLossDB = pl0 + 10.0*n*math.Log10(dTotal/1.0)\n637\t\t} else {\n638\t\t\tpathLossDB = pl0\n639\t\t}\n640\t\n641\t\t// Convert to linear amplitude\n642\t\tamplitude := math.Pow(10.0, -pathLossDB/20.0)\n643\t\n644\t\t// Apply Fresnel zone decay\n645\t\tamplitude *= decay\n646\t\n647\t\t// Phase at this position (based on total path length)\n648\t\tphase := 2 * math.Pi * dTotal / wavelength\n649\t\n650\t\treturn amplitude, phase\n651\t}\n652\t\n653\t// distance computes Euclidean distance between two points\n654\tfunc distance(a, b Point) float64 {\n655\t\tdx := a.X - b.X\n656\t\tdy := a.Y - b.Y\n657\t\tdz := a.Z - b.Z\n658\t\treturn math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz)\n659\t}\n660\t\n661\t// amplitudeToRSSI converts amplitude to RSSI in dBm\n662\tfunc amplitudeToRSSI(amplitude float64) int8 {\n663\t\t// Convert amplitude to dBm (reference: amplitude 1.0 = -30 dBm)\n664\t\tamplitudeDBm := -30.0 + 20.0*math.Log10(amplitude)\n665\t\n666\t\t// Clamp to realistic range\n667\t\tif amplitudeDBm < -90 {\n668\t\t\tamplitudeDBm = -90\n669\t\t}\n670\t\tif amplitudeDBm > -30 {\n671\t\t\tamplitudeDBm = -30\n672\t\t}\n673\t\n674\t\treturn int8(amplitudeDBm)\n675\t}\n676\t\n677\t// generateIQPair generates a synthetic I/Q pair\n678\tfunc generateIQPair(amplitude, phase float64, rng *rand.Rand) (float64, float64) {\n679\t\ti := amplitude * math.Cos(phase)\n680\t\tq := amplitude * math.Sin(phase)\n681\t\treturn i, q\n682\t}\n683\t\n684\t// randNorm generates a normally-distributed random value (Box-Muller)\n685\tfunc randNorm(rng *rand.Rand, mean, stddev float64) float64 {\n686\t\tu1 := rng.Float64()\n687\t\tu2 := rng.Float64()\n688\t\tz0 := math.Sqrt(-2.0*math.Log(u1)) * math.Cos(2.0*math.Pi*u2)\n689\t\treturn mean + stddev*z0\n690\t}\n691\t\n692\t// updateWalkers updates walker positions using random walk behavior\n693\tfunc updateWalkers(walkers []*Walker, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) {\n694\t\tconst dt = 0.05 // 50ms in seconds\n695\t\tconst sigma = 0.3 // m/s per axis\n696\t\n697\t\tfor _, walker := range walkers {\n698\t\t\t// Gaussian velocity update\n699\t\t\tdvx := randNorm(rng, 0, sigma)\n700\t\t\tdvy := randNorm(rng, 0, sigma)\n701\t\t\tdvz := randNorm(rng, 0, sigma)\n702\t\n703\t\t\twalker.Velocity.X += dvx * dt\n704\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Y += dvy * dt\n705\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Z += dvz * dt\n706\t\n707\t\t\t// Clamp velocity to reasonable range\n708\t\t\tmaxV := 2.0 // m/s\n709\t\t\tvMag := math.Sqrt(walker.Velocity.X*walker.Velocity.X +\n710\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Y*walker.Velocity.Y +\n711\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Z*walker.Velocity.Z)\n712\t\t\tif vMag > maxV {\n713\t\t\t\tscale := maxV / vMag\n714\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.X *= scale\n715\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Y *= scale\n716\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Z *= scale\n717\t\t\t}\n718\t\n719\t\t\t// Update position\n720\t\t\twalker.Position.X += walker.Velocity.X * dt\n721\t\t\twalker.Position.Y += walker.Velocity.Y * dt\n722\t\t\twalker.Position.Z += walker.Velocity.Z * dt\n723\t\n724\t\t\t// Reflect at walls\n725\t\t\tif walker.Position.X < 0 {\n726\t\t\t\twalker.Position.X = -walker.Position.X\n727\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.X *= -1\n728\t\t\t}\n729\t\t\tif walker.Position.X > space.Width {\n730\t\t\t\twalker.Position.X = 2*space.Width - walker.Position.X\n731\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.X *= -1\n732\t\t\t}\n733\t\t\tif walker.Position.Y < 0 {\n734\t\t\t\twalker.Position.Y = -walker.Position.Y\n735\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Y *= -1\n736\t\t\t}\n737\t\t\tif walker.Position.Y > space.Depth {\n738\t\t\t\twalker.Position.Y = 2*space.Depth - walker.Position.Y\n739\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Y *= -1\n740\t\t\t}\n741\t\t\tif walker.Position.Z < 0 {\n742\t\t\t\twalker.Position.Z = -walker.Position.Z\n743\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Z *= -1\n744\t\t\t}\n745\t\t\tif walker.Position.Z > space.Height {\n746\t\t\t\twalker.Position.Z = 2*space.Height - walker.Position.Z\n747\t\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Z *= -1\n748\t\t\t}\n749\t\t}\n750\t}\n751\t\n752\t// sendBLEAdvertisements sends simulated BLE advertisements from one node\n753\tfunc sendBLEAdvertisements(nodes []*VirtualNode, rng *rand.Rand) {\n754\t\tif len(nodes) == 0 {\n755\t\t\treturn\n756\t\t}\n757\t\n758\t\t// Send from first node\n759\t\tnode := nodes[0]\n760\t\n761\t\tnode.mu.Lock()\n762\t\tconn := node.Conn\n763\t\tnode.mu.Unlock()\n764\t\n765\t\tif conn == nil {\n766\t\t\treturn\n767\t\t}\n768\t\n769\t\t// Generate simulated BLE device address\n770\t\taddr := fmt.Sprintf(\"AA:BB:CC:DD:%02X:%02X\", rng.Intn(256), rng.Intn(256))\n771\t\trssi := -60 + rng.Intn(20) // -60 to -40 dBm\n772\t\n773\t\tble := map[string]interface{}{\n774\t\t\t\"type\": \"ble\",\n775\t\t\t\"mac\":  macToString(node.MAC),\n776\t\t\t\"devices\": []map[string]interface{}{\n777\t\t\t\t{\n778\t\t\t\t\t\"addr\": conn.WriteJSON(ble); err != nil {\n787\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Failed to send BLE advertisement: %v\", err)\n788\t\t}\n789\t}\n790\t\n791\t// pollBlobCount polls the mothership for blob count\n792\tfunc pollBlobCount() {\n793\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)\n794\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n795\t\n796\t\tfor range ticker.C {\n797\t\t\t// Build HTTP URL from WebSocket URL\n798\t\t\twsURL, err := url.Parse(*flagMothership)\n799\t\t\tif err != nil {\n800\t\t\t\tcontinue\n801\t\t\t}\n802\t\n803\t\t\thttpURL := *wsURL\n804\t\t\tif httpURL.Scheme == \"ws\" {\n805\t\t\t\thttpURL.Scheme = \"http\"\n806\t\t\t} else if httpURL.Scheme == \"wss\" {\n807\t\t\t\thttpURL.Scheme = \"https\"\n808\t\t\t}\n809\t\n810\t\t\tblobsURL := httpURL.String()\n811\t\t\tblobsURL = strings.TrimSuffix(blobsURL, \"/ws\")\n812\t\t\tblobsURL = strings.TrimSuffix(blobsURL, \"/\")\n813\t\t\tblobsURL += \"/api/blobs\"\n814\t\n815\t\t\tresp, err := http.Get(blobsURL)\n816\t\t\tif err != nil {\n817\t\t\t\tcontinue\n818\t\t\t}\n819\t\n820\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK {\n821\t\t\t\tvar blobs []json.RawMessage\n822\t\t\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&blobs); err == nil {\n823\t\t\t\t\tstats.BlobCount = len(blobs)\n824\t\t\t\t}\n825\t\t\t}\n826\t\t\tresp.Body.Close()\n827\t\t}\n828\t}\n829\t\n830\t// reportStats reports statistics every second\n831\tfunc reportStats() {\n832\t\tticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)\n833\t\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n834\t\n835\t\tfor range ticker.C {\n836\t\t\tnow := time.Now()\n837\t\t\telapsed := now.Sub(stats.LastStatsTime).Seconds()\n838\t\t\tif elapsed < 1 {\n839\t\t\t\tcontinue\n840\t\t\t}\n841\t\n842\t\t\tframesSent := stats.FramesSent.Load()\n843\t\t\tframesInPeriod := framesSent - stats.LastFramesSent\n844\t\n845\t\t\tstats.FramesPerSec = float64(framesInPeriod) / elapsed\n846\t\n847\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Stats: frames/s=%.1f total=%d blobs=%d\",\n848\t\t\t\tstats.FramesPerSec, framesSent, stats.BlobCount)\n849\t\n850\t\t\tstats.LastStatsTime = now\n851\t\t\tstats.LastFramesSent = framesSent\n852\t\t}\n853\t}\n854\t\n855\t// printFinalStats prints final simulation statistics\n856\tfunc printFinalStats() {\n857\t\telapsed := time.Since(stats.StartTime).Seconds()\n858\t\tframesSent := stats.FramesSent.Load()\n859\t\n860\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Final Statistics:\")\n861\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Frames sent: %d\", framesSent)\n862\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Duration: %.1f seconds\", elapsed)\n863\t\tif elapsed > 0 {\n864\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Average FPS: %.1f\", float64(framesSent)/elapsed)\n865\t\t}\n866\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Blobs detected: %d\", stats.BlobCount)\n867\t}\n868\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5ddf1773-ae76-4160-9d11-f3b1a575141e","uuid":"4b9b9dca-d66f-4f74-b78d-1740e8c7f4fb","timestamp":"2026-07-07T10:51:13.974Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/cmd/sim/main.go","content":"// (\n\t// CSI frame header size (24 bytes) — matches ingestion/frame.go\n\theaderSize = 24\n\n\t// Default values\n\tdefaultMothership = \"ws://localhost:8080/ws/node\"\n\tdefaultNodes      = flags\n\tflagMothership = flag.String(\"mothership\", defaultMothership, \"URL of the mothership WebSocket endpoint\")\n\tflagToken      = flag.String(\"token\", \"\", \"Provisioning token (auto-generated if empty)\")\n\tflagNodes      = flag.Int(\"nodes\", defaultNodes, \"Number of virtual nodes\")\n\tflagWalkers    = flag.Int(\"walkers\", defaultWalkers, \"Number of synthetic walkers\")\n\tflagRate       = flag.Int(\"rate\", defaultRate, \"CSI transmission rate in Hz per node pair\")\n\tflagDuration   = flag.Int(\"duration\", defaultDuration, \"Total run time in seconds (0 = run forever)\")\n\tflagBLE        = flag.Bool(\"ble\", false, \"Include simulated BLE advertisements\")\n\tflagSeed       = flag.Int64(\"seed\", defaultSeed, \"Random seed for reproducible runs\")\n\tflagSpace      = flag.String(\"space\", defaultSpace, \"Room dimensions in WxDxH format (meters)\")\n)\n\n// VirtualNode represents a simulated ESP32 node\ntype VirtualNode struct {\n\tID       int\n\tMAC      [6]byte\n\tPosition Point\n\tConn     *websocket.Conn\n\tmu       sync.Mutex\n}\n\n// Walker represents a simulated person\ntype Walker struct {\n\tID       int\n\tPosition Point\n\tVelocity Point\n}\n\n// Point represents a 3D position\ntype Point struct {\n\tX, Y, Z float64\n}\n\n// Space represents the room dimensions\ntype Space struct {\n\tWidth, Depth, Height float64\n}\n\n// Stats tracks simulation statistics\ntype Stats struct {\n\tFramesSent     atomic.Int64\n\tFramesPerSec   float64\n\tStartTime      time.Time\n\tLastStatsTime  time.Time\n\tLastFramesSent int64\n\tBlobCount      int\n\tRejected       atomic.Bool // Set to true when any node is rejected\n}\n\nvar stats Stats\n\nfunc main() {\n\tflag.Parse()\n\n\tlog.SetFlags(log.Ldate | log.Ltime | log.Lmicroseconds)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] CSI Simulator starting\")\n\n\t// Parse space dimensions\n\tspace, err := parseSpace(*flagSpace)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Invalid space dimensions: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Initialize random seed\n\trng := rand.New(rand.NewSource(*flagSeed))\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Random seed: %d\", *flagSeed)\n\n\t// Generate or validate token\n\ttoken := *flagToken\n\tif token == \"\" {\n\t\t// For testing, generate a dummy token\n\t\t// In production, this should be derived from the install secret\n\t\ttoken = fmt.Sprintf(\"%064x\", rng.Uint64())\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Auto-generated token (first 16 chars): %s...\", token[:16])\n\t}\n\n\t// Create virtual nodes at fixed positions (corners, evenly distributed)\n\tnodes := createVirtualNodes(*flagNodes, space, rng)\n\n\t// Create walkers with random walk behavior\n\twalkers := createWalkers(*flagWalkers, space, rng)\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Configuration:\")\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Mothership: %s\", *flagMothership)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Nodes: %d\", *flagNodes)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Walkers: %d\", *flagWalkers)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Rate: %d Hz\", *flagRate)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Duration: %d s\", *flagDuration)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Space: %.1fx%.1fx%.1f m\", space.Width, space.Depth, space.Height)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   BLE: %v\", *flagBLE)\n\n\t// Create context for shutdown\n\tctx, cancel := contextWithCancel()\n\tdefer cancel()\n\n\t// Channel for reject notifications\n\trejectChan := make(chan struct{}, len(nodes))\n\n\t// Connect all nodes to mothership\n\tif err := connectNodes(ctx, nodes, token, rng, rejectChan); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Fatalf(\"[SIM] Failed to connect nodes: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Start blob count polling\n\tgo pollBlobCount()\n\n\t// Start stats reporting\n\tgo reportStats()\n\n\t// Start simulation (monitor for reject)\n\trunSimulation(ctx, nodes, walkers, space, rng, rejectChan)\n\n\t// Shutdown\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Shutting down...\")\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\tnode.mu.Lock()\n\t\tif node.Conn != nil {\n\t\t\tnode.Conn.Close()\n\t\t}\n\t\tnode.mu.Unlock()\n\t}\n\n\t// Print final statistics\n\tprintFinalStats()\n\n\t// Exit non-zero if rejected\n\tif stats.Rejected.Load() {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Exiting due to rejection\")\n\t\tos.Exit(1)\n\t}\n}\n\n// parseSpace parses space dimensions from \"WxDxH\" format\nfunc parseSpace(s string) (*Space, error) {\n\tvar w, d, h float64\n\t_, err := fmt.Sscanf(s, \"%fx%fx%f\", &w, &d, &h)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid format (expected WxDxH): %w\", err)\n\t}\n\tif w <= 0 || d <= 0 || h <= 0 {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"dimensions must be positive\")\n\t}\n\treturn &Space{Width: w, Depth: d, Height: h}, nil\n}\n\n// createVirtualNodes creates virtual nodes at corners, evenly distributed\nfunc createVirtualNodes(count int, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) []*VirtualNode {\n\tnodes := make([]*VirtualNode, count)\n\n\t// Position nodes at corners and midpoints\n\tpositions := generateNodePositions(count, space)\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\tmac := generateMAC(i)\n\t\tnodes[i] = &VirtualNode{\n\t\t\tID:       i,\n\t\t\tMAC:      mac,\n\t\t\tPosition: positions[i],\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: MAC=%s pos=(%.2f,%.2f,%.2f)\",\n\t\t\ti, macToString(mac), positions[i].X, positions[i].Y, positions[i].Z)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nodes\n}\n\n// generateNodePositions generates positions for nodes evenly distributed in the space\nfunc generateNodePositions(count int, space *Space) []Point {\n\tpositions := make([]Point, count)\n\n\t// For small counts, use corners\n\t// For larger counts, distribute evenly\n\tif count == 1 {\n\t\tpositions[0] = Point{X: space.Width / 2, Y: space.Depth / 2, Z: space.Height / 2}\n\t} else if count == 2 {\n\t\tpositions[0] = Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n\t\tpositions[1] = Point{X: space.Width, Y: space.Depth, Z: space.Height}\n\t} else if count == 3 {\n\t\tpositions[0] = Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n\t\tpositions[1] = Point{X: space.Width, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n\t\tpositions[2] = Point{X: space.Width / 2, Y: space.Depth, Z: 0}\n\t} else if count == 4 {\n\t\tpositions[0] = Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n\t\tpositions[1] = Point{X: space.Width, Y: 0, Z: space.Height}\n\t\tpositions[2] = Point{X: 0, Y: space.Depth, Z: space.Height}\n\t\tpositions[3] = Point{X: space.Width, Y: space.Depth, Z: space.Height}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// For more than 4 nodes, distribute in a grid pattern\n\t\tgridSize := int(math.Ceil(math.Sqrt(float64(count))))\n\t\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\t\trow := i / gridSize\n\t\t\tcol := i % gridSize\n\t\t\tpositions[i] = Point{\n\t\t\t\tX: float64(col) * space.Width / float64(gridSize-1),\n\t\t\t\tY: float64(row) * space.Depth / float64(gridSize-1),\n\t\t\t\tZ: space.Height / 2,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn positions\n}\n\n// generateMAC generates a MAC address for a virtual node\nfunc generateMAC(id int) [6]byte {\n\tvar mac [6]byte\n\t// Use a predictable OUI + node ID\n\tmac[0] = 0x02 // Locally administered\n\tmac[1] = 0x53 // Spaxel OUI (fictional)\n\tmac[2] = 0xAC\n\tmac[3] = byte((id >> 16) & 0xFF)\n\tmac[4] = byte((id >> 8) & 0xFF)\n\tmac[5] = byte(id & 0xFF)\n\treturn mac\n}\n\n// macToString converts a 6-byte MAC to colon-separated hex\nfunc macToString(mac [6]byte) string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X:%02X\",\n\t\tmac[0], mac[1], mac[2], mac[3], mac[4], mac[5])\n}\n\n// createWalkers creates walkers with random walk behavior\nfunc createWalkers(count int, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) []*Walker {\n\twalkers := make([]*Walker, count)\n\tfor i := 0; i < count; i++ {\n\t\twalkers[i] = &Walker{\n\t\t\tID:       i,\n\t\t\tPosition: Point{X: space.Width / 2, Y: space.Depth / 2, Z: 1.0}, // Start in center\n\t\t\tVelocity: Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: 0},\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Walker %d: starting at (%.2f,%.2f,%.2f)\",\n\t\t\ti, walkers[i].Position.X, walkers[i].Position.Y, walkers[i].Position.Z)\n\t}\n\treturn walkers\n}\n\n// contextWithCancel creates a context that can be cancelled\nfunc contextWithCancel() (context.Context, context.CancelFunc) {\n\treturn context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n}\n\n// connectNodes connects all virtual nodes to the mothership via WebSocket\nfunc connectNodes(ctx context.Context, nodes []*VirtualNode, token string, rng *rand.Rand, rejectChan chan<- struct{}) error {\n\tvar wg sync.WaitGroup\n\terrChan := make(chan error, len(nodes))\n\n\tfor _, node := range nodes {\n\t\twg.Add(1)\n\t\tgo func(n *VirtualNode) {\n\t\t\tdefer wg.Done()\n\n\t\t\t// Build WebSocket URL with token in header\n\t\t\tu, err := url.Parse(*flagMothership)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"node %d: invalid URL: %w\", n.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Create request with token header\n\t\t\treqHeader := http.Header{}\n\t\t\treqHeader.Set(\"X-Spaxel-Token\", token)\n\n\t\t\t// Connect to WebSocket\n\t\t\tconn, resp, err := websocket.DefaultDialer.DialContext(ctx, u.String(), reqHeader)\n\t\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tif resp != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Check for reject response\n\t\t\t\t\tif resp.StatusCode == http.StatusUnauthorized || resp.StatusCode == http.StatusForbidden {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tresp.Body.Close()\n\t\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: REJECT response from mothership (status %d): %s\", n.ID, resp.StatusCode, string(body))\n\t\t\t\t\t\tstats.Rejected.Store(true)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase rejectChan <- struct{}{}:\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"node %d: rejected by mothership\", n.ID)\n\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\tresp.Body.Close()\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\terrChan <- fmt.Errorf(\"node %d: connection failed: %w\", n.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdefer resp.Body.Close()\n\n\t\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n\t\t\tn.Conn = conn\n\t\t\tn.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: connected to mothership\", n.ID)\n\n\t\t\t// Send hello message. Announce the node's computed position\n\t\t\t// (createVirtualNodes corner geometry) so the mothership persists it\n\t\t\t// in the fleet/DB row instead of leaving it at the schema default (bf-24xp).\n\t\t\thello := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\t\"type\":            \"hello\",\n\t\t\t\t\"mac\":             macToString(n.MAC),\n\t\t\t\t\"firmware_version\": \"sim-1.0.0\",\n\t\t\t\t\"capabilities\":    []string{\"csi\", \"ble\", \"tx\", \"rx\"},\n\t\t\t\t\"chip\":            \"ESP32-S3\",\n\t\t\t\t\"flash_mb\":        16,\n\t\t\t\t\"uptime_ms\":       1000,\n\t\t\t\t\"pos_x\":           n.Position.X,\n\t\t\t\t\"pos_y\":           n.Position.Y,\n\t\t\t\t\"pos_z\":           n.Position.Z,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif err := conn.WriteJSON(hello); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: failed to send hello: %v\", n.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\terrChan <- err\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Listen for downstream messages (role assignment, config, reject)\n\t\t\tgo n.listenForDownstream(ctx, rejectChan)\n\t\t}(node)\n\t}\n\n\twg.Wait()\n\tclose(errChan)\n\n\t// Check for errors\n\tvar errs []error\n\tfor err := range errChan {\n\t\terrs = append(errs, err)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"connection errors: %v\", errs)\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// listenForDownstream listens for downstream messages from the mothership\nfunc (n *VirtualNode) listenForDownstream(ctx context.Context, rejectChan chan<- struct{}) {\n\tn.mu.Lock()\n\tconn := n.Conn\n\tn.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tn.mu.Lock()\n\t\tif n.Conn == conn {\n\t\t\tn.Conn = nil\n\t\t}\n\t\tn.mu.Unlock()\n\t\tconn.Close()\n\t}()\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar msg json.RawMessage\n\t\tif err := conn.ReadJSON(&msg); err != nil {\n\t\t\tif ctx.Err() != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: read error: %v\", n.ID, err)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Parse message type\n\t\tvar typeMsg struct {\n\t\t\tType string `json:\"type\"`\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(msg, &typeMsg); err != nil {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: invalid message: %s\", n.ID, string(msg))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tswitch typeMsg.Type {\n\t\tcase \"reject\":\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: REJECT message received: %s\", n.ID, string(msg))\n\t\t\tstats.Rejected.Store(true)\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase rejectChan <- struct{}{}:\n\t\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase \"role\", \"config\":\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: received %s message\", n.ID, typeMsg.Type)\n\t\tcase \"ota\", \"reboot\", \"identify\", \"baseline_request\":\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: received %s message (acknowledged)\", n.ID, typeMsg.Type)\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: received unknown message type: %s\", n.ID, typeMsg.Type)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// runSimulation runs the main simulation loop\nfunc runSimulation(ctx context.Context, nodes []*VirtualNode, walkers []*Walker, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand, rejectChan <-chan struct{}) {\n\tstats.StartTime = time.Now()\n\tstats.LastStatsTime = stats.StartTime\n\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(time.Duration(1000/(*flagRate)) * time.Millisecond)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tbleTicker := time.NewTicker(5 * time.Second)\n\tdefer bleTicker.Stop()\n\n\tdurationTimer := time.NewTimer(time.Duration(*flagDuration) * time.Second)\n\tif *flagDuration == 0 {\n\t\tdurationTimer.Stop()\n\t}\n\n\tframeNum := 0\n\twalkerUpdateTicker := time.NewTicker(50 * time.Millisecond) // Update walkers every 50ms\n\tdefer walkerUpdateTicker.Stop()\n\n\t// Handle interrupt signal\n\tsigChan := make(chan os.Signal, 1)\n\tsignal.Notify(sigChan, os.Interrupt)\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-sigChan:\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Interrupted, shutting down...\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-durationTimer.C:\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Duration elapsed, shutting down...\")\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-rejectChan:\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node rejected by mothership, exiting...\")\n\t\t\tstats.Rejected.Store(true)\n\t\t\treturn\n\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n\t\t\t// Send CSI frames for all TX->RX pairs\n\t\t\tfor _, tx := range nodes {\n\t\t\t\tfor _, rx := range nodes {\n\t\t\t\t\tif tx.ID == rx.ID {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue // Skip self-pairs\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t\tframe := generateCSIFrame(tx, rx, walkers, frameNum, rng)\n\n\t\t\t\t\ttx.mu.Lock()\n\t\t\t\t\tconn := tx.Conn\n\t\t\t\t\ttx.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t\t\t\t\tif conn != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tif err := conn.WriteMessage(websocket.BinaryMessage, frame); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Node %d: send error: %v\", tx.ID, err)\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t\t\tstats.FramesSent.Add(1)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tframeNum++\n\t\tcase <-walkerUpdateTicker.C:\n\t\t\t// Update walker positions (random walk)\n\t\t\tupdateWalkers(walkers, space, rng)\n\t\tcase <-bleTicker.C:\n\t\t\tif *flagBLE {\n\t\t\t\tsendBLEAdvertisements(nodes, rng)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// generateCSIFrame generates a synthetic CSI binary frame\nfunc generateCSIFrame(tx, rx *VirtualNode, walkers []*Walker, frameNum int, rng *rand.Rand) []byte {\n\t// Calculate combined CSI from all walkers\n\tamplitude, phaseBase := computeCSIForWalkers(tx, rx, walkers)\n\n\t// Compute RSSI from amplitude\n\trssi := amplitudeToRSSI(amplitude)\n\n\t// Create frame buffer\n\tframe := make([]byte, headerSize+nSub*2)\n\n\t// Write header (matches ingestion/frame.go ParseFrame layout)\n\tcopy(frame[0:6], tx.MAC[:])   // node_mac\n\tcopy(frame[6:12], rx.MAC[:])  // peer_mac\n\tbinary.LittleEndian.PutUint64(frame[12:20], uint64(frameNum*50000)) // timestamp_us\n\tframe[20] = byte(int8(rssi))  // rssi\n\tframe[21] = byte(-95 & 0xFF)   // noise_floor: -95 dBm\n\tframe[22] = byte(defaultChannel) // channel\n\tframe[23] = nSub              // n_sub\n\n\t// Generate I/Q pairs for each subcarrier\n\tfor k := 0; k < nSub; k++ {\n\t\t// Phase for this subcarrier\n\t\tphase := phaseBase + float64(k)*0.1\n\n\t\t// Add temporal variation\n\t\tphase += 0.1 * math.Sin(2*math.Pi*float64(frameNum)/100.0)\n\n\t\t// Normalize phase to [-π, π]\n\t\tfor phase > math.Pi {\n\t\t\tphase -= 2 * math.Pi\n\t\t}\n\t\tfor phase < -math.Pi {\n\t\t\tphase += 2 * math.Pi\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Add frequency-selective fading\n\t\tfreqFading := 0.8 + 0.4*math.Sin(2*math.Pi*float64(k)/16.0)\n\t\tsubAmplitude := amplitude * freqFading\n\n\t\t// Add Gaussian noise\n\t\tamplitudeNoisy := subAmplitude * (1 + randNorm(rng, 0, 0.05))\n\n\t\t// Generate I/Q\n\t\ti, q := generateIQPair(amplitudeNoisy, phase, rng)\n\n\t\t// Write to payload (interleaved I,Q)\n\t\toffset := headerSize + k*2\n\t\tframe[offset] = byte(int8(i))\n\t\tframe[offset+1] = byte(int8(q))\n\t}\n\n\treturn frame\n}\n\n// computeCSIForWalkers computes the combined CSI amplitude and phase from all walkers\nfunc computeCSIForWalkers(tx, rx *VirtualNode, walkers []*Walker) (float64, float64) {\n\tif len(walkers) == 0 {\n\t\t// No walkers, return baseline noise\n\t\treturn 0.001, 0.0\n\t}\n\n\tvar totalAmplitude float64\n\tvar totalPhase float64\n\tvar weight float64\n\n\tfor _, walker := range walkers {\n\t\t// Direct path contribution\n\t\tdirectAmp, directPhase := computeDirectPath(tx.Position, rx.Position, walker.Position)\n\n\t\t// Scale to reasonable values\n\t\tcombinedAmp := directAmp * 1000.0\n\n\t\t// Accumulate\n\t\ttotalAmplitude += combinedAmp\n\t\ttotalPhase += directPhase\n\t\tweight += 1.0\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize phase\n\tif weight > 0 {\n\t\ttotalPhase /= weight\n\t}\n\n\treturn totalAmplitude, totalPhase\n}\n\n// computeDirectPath computes the CSI contribution from the direct path\nfunc computeDirectPath(tx, rx, walker Point) (float64, float64) {\n\t// Distance from TX to walker\n\td1 := distance(tx, walker)\n\t// Distance from walker to RX\n\td2 := distance(walker, rx)\n\t// Total path length\n\tdTotal := d1 + d2\n\n\t// Direct TX-RX distance (for Fresnel zone calculation)\n\tdDirect := distance(tx, rx)\n\n\t// Path length excess for Fresnel zone calculation\n\texcess := dTotal - dDirect\n\tif excess < 0 {\n\t\texcess = 0\n\t}\n\n\t// Fresnel zone number\n\tzoneNumber := int(math.Ceil(excess / halfWavelength))\n\tif zoneNumber < 1 {\n\t\tzoneNumber = 1\n\t}\n\n\t// Zone decay (inverse square)\n\tdecay := 1.0 / math.Pow(float64(zoneNumber), 2.0)\n\n\t// Log-distance path loss model: PL(d) = PL_0 + 10*n*log10(d/d_0)\n\tvar pathLossDB float64\n\tif dTotal >= 1.0 {\n\t\tpathLossDB = pl0 + 10.0*n*math.Log10(dTotal/1.0)\n\t} else {\n\t\tpathLossDB = pl0\n\t}\n\n\t// Convert to linear amplitude\n\tamplitude := math.Pow(10.0, -pathLossDB/20.0)\n\n\t// Apply Fresnel zone decay\n\tamplitude *= decay\n\n\t// Phase at this position (based on total path length)\n\tphase := 2 * math.Pi * dTotal / wavelength\n\n\treturn amplitude, phase\n}\n\n// distance computes Euclidean distance between two points\nfunc distance(a, b Point) float64 {\n\tdx := a.X - b.X\n\tdy := a.Y - b.Y\n\tdz := a.Z - b.Z\n\treturn math.Sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy + dz*dz)\n}\n\n// amplitudeToRSSI converts amplitude to RSSI in dBm\nfunc amplitudeToRSSI(amplitude float64) int8 {\n\t// Convert amplitude to dBm (reference: amplitude 1.0 = -30 dBm)\n\tamplitudeDBm := -30.0 + 20.0*math.Log10(amplitude)\n\n\t// Clamp to realistic range\n\tif amplitudeDBm < -90 {\n\t\tamplitudeDBm = -90\n\t}\n\tif amplitudeDBm > -30 {\n\t\tamplitudeDBm = -30\n\t}\n\n\treturn int8(amplitudeDBm)\n}\n\n// generateIQPair generates a synthetic I/Q pair\nfunc generateIQPair(amplitude, phase float64, rng *rand.Rand) (float64, float64) {\n\ti := amplitude * math.Cos(phase)\n\tq := amplitude * math.Sin(phase)\n\treturn i, q\n}\n\n// randNorm generates a normally-distributed random value (Box-Muller)\nfunc randNorm(rng *rand.Rand, mean, stddev float64) float64 {\n\tu1 := rng.Float64()\n\tu2 := rng.Float64()\n\tz0 := math.Sqrt(-2.0*math.Log(u1)) * math.Cos(2.0*math.Pi*u2)\n\treturn mean + stddev*z0\n}\n\n// updateWalkers updates walker positions using random walk behavior\nfunc updateWalkers(walkers []*Walker, space *Space, rng *rand.Rand) {\n\tconst dt = 0.05 // 50ms in seconds\n\tconst sigma = 0.3 // m/s per axis\n\n\tfor _, walker := range walkers {\n\t\t// Gaussian velocity update\n\t\tdvx := randNorm(rng, 0, sigma)\n\t\tdvy := randNorm(rng, 0, sigma)\n\t\tdvz := randNorm(rng, 0, sigma)\n\n\t\twalker.Velocity.X += dvx * dt\n\t\twalker.Velocity.Y += dvy * dt\n\t\twalker.Velocity.Z += dvz * dt\n\n\t\t// Clamp velocity to reasonable range\n\t\tmaxV := 2.0 // m/s\n\t\tvMag := math.Sqrt(walker.Velocity.X*walker.Velocity.X +\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Y*walker.Velocity.Y +\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Z*walker.Velocity.Z)\n\t\tif vMag > maxV {\n\t\t\tscale := maxV / vMag\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.X *= scale\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Y *= scale\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Z *= scale\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Update position\n\t\twalker.Position.X += walker.Velocity.X * dt\n\t\twalker.Position.Y += walker.Velocity.Y * dt\n\t\twalker.Position.Z += walker.Velocity.Z * dt\n\n\t\t// Reflect at walls\n\t\tif walker.Position.X < 0 {\n\t\t\twalker.Position.X = -walker.Position.X\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.X *= -1\n\t\t}\n\t\tif walker.Position.X > space.Width {\n\t\t\twalker.Position.X = 2*space.Width - walker.Position.X\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.X *= -1\n\t\t}\n\t\tif walker.Position.Y < 0 {\n\t\t\twalker.Position.Y = -walker.Position.Y\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Y *= -1\n\t\t}\n\t\tif walker.Position.Y > space.Depth {\n\t\t\twalker.Position.Y = 2*space.Depth - walker.Position.Y\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Y *= -1\n\t\t}\n\t\tif walker.Position.Z < 0 {\n\t\t\twalker.Position.Z = -walker.Position.Z\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Z *= -1\n\t\t}\n\t\tif walker.Position.Z > space.Height {\n\t\t\twalker.Position.Z = 2*space.Height - walker.Position.Z\n\t\t\twalker.Velocity.Z *= -1\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// sendBLEAdvertisements sends simulated BLE advertisements from one node\nfunc sendBLEAdvertisements(nodes []*VirtualNode, rng *rand.Rand) {\n\tif len(nodes) == 0 {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Send from first node\n\tnode := nodes[0]\n\n\tnode.mu.Lock()\n\tconn := node.Conn\n\tnode.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tif conn == nil {\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Generate simulated BLE device address\n\taddr := fmt.Sprintf(\"AA:BB:CC:DD:%02X:%02X\", rng.Intn(256), rng.Intn(256))\n\trssi := -60 + rng.Intn(20) // -60 to -40 dBm\n\n\tble := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"type\": \"ble\",\n\t\t\"mac\":  macToString(node.MAC),\n\t\t\"devices\": []map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\t\"addr\":        addr,\n\t\t\t\t\"addr_type\":   \"random\",\n\t\t\t\t\"rssi_dbm\":    rssi,\n\t\t\t\t\"name\":        \"SimPerson\",\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tif err := conn.WriteJSON(ble); err != nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Failed to send BLE advertisement: %v\", err)\n\t}\n}\n\n// pollBlobCount polls the mothership for blob count\nfunc pollBlobCount() {\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tfor range ticker.C {\n\t\t// Build HTTP URL from WebSocket URL\n\t\twsURL, err := url.Parse(*flagMothership)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\thttpURL := *wsURL\n\t\tif httpURL.Scheme == \"ws\" {\n\t\t\thttpURL.Scheme = \"http\"\n\t\t} else if httpURL.Scheme == \"wss\" {\n\t\t\thttpURL.Scheme = \"https\"\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tblobsURL := httpURL.String()\n\t\tblobsURL = strings.TrimSuffix(blobsURL, \"/ws\")\n\t\tblobsURL = strings.TrimSuffix(blobsURL, \"/\")\n\t\tblobsURL += \"/api/blobs\"\n\n\t\tresp, err := http.Get(blobsURL)\n\t\tif err != nil {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif resp.StatusCode == http.StatusOK {\n\t\t\tvar blobs []json.RawMessage\n\t\t\tif err := json.NewDecoder(resp.Body).Decode(&blobs); err == nil {\n\t\t\t\tstats.BlobCount = len(blobs)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tresp.Body.Close()\n\t}\n}\n\n// reportStats reports statistics every second\nfunc reportStats() {\n\tticker := time.NewTicker(1 * time.Second)\n\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n\n\tfor range ticker.C {\n\t\tnow := time.Now()\n\t\telapsed := now.Sub(stats.LastStatsTime).Seconds()\n\t\tif elapsed < 1 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tframesSent := stats.FramesSent.Load()\n\t\tframesInPeriod := framesSent - stats.LastFramesSent\n\n\t\tstats.FramesPerSec = float64(framesInPeriod) / elapsed\n\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Stats: frames/s=%.1f total=%d blobs=%d\",\n\t\t\tstats.FramesPerSec, framesSent, stats.BlobCount)\n\n\t\tstats.LastStatsTime = now\n\t\tstats.LastFramesSent = framesSent\n\t}\n}\n\n// printFinalStats prints final simulation statistics\nfunc printFinalStats() {\n\telapsed := time.Since(stats.StartTime).Seconds()\n\tframesSent := stats.FramesSent.Load()\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM] Final Statistics:\")\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Frames sent: %d\", framesSent)\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Duration: %.1f seconds\", elapsed)\n\tif elapsed > 0 {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Average FPS: %.1f\", float64(framesSent)/elapsed)\n\t}\n\tlog.Printf(\"[SIM]   Blobs detected: %d\", stats.BlobCount)\n}\n","numLines":868,"startLine":1,"totalLines":868}}}

It is supplied to the mothership\n42\texclusively via this HTTP header on the upgrade request. There is no second\n43\tdelivery channel.\n44\t\n45\t## Fact 2 — the hello JSON body has NO `token` field\n46\t\n47\tThe hello map built and sent as the node's first WS frame omits `token`\n48\tentirely:\n49\t\n50\t- `cmd/sim/main.go:348-359`:\n51\t  ```\n52\t  hello := map[string]interface{}{\n53\t      \"type\":            \"hello\",\n54\t      \"mac\":             macToString(n.MAC),\n55\t      \"firmware_version\": \"sim-1.0.0\",\n56\t      \"capabilities\":    []string{\"csi\", \"ble\", \"tx\", \"rx\"},\n57\t      \"chip\":            \"ESP32-S3\",\n58\t      \"flash_mb\":        16,\n59\t      \"uptime_ms\":       1000,\n60\t      \"pos_x\":           n.Position.X,\n61\t      \"pos_y\":           n.Position.Y,\n62\t      \"pos_z\":           n.Position.Z,\n63\t  }\n64\t  ```\n65\t- `mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:652-665`: keys are\n66\t  `type, mac, firmware_version, capabilities, chip, flash_mb, uptime_ms,\n67\t   wifi_rssi, ip, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z`.\n68\t- `mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:331-342`: keys are\n69\t  `type, mac, firmware_version, capabilities, chip, flash_mb, uptime_ms,\n70\t   wifi_rssi, ip`.\n71\t\n72\tNone of the three contains a `token` key. Since the body never sets\n85\t`token`, that field deserializes to `\"\"` from either sim binary.\n86\t\n87\t## Fact 3 — the mothership NEVER reads `X-Spaxel-Token` on the WS path\n88\t\n89\tA grep for the literal header name across the entire mothership tree,\n90\texcluding the sim sources that *set* it and the test files that document the\n91\tcontract, returns **zero** hits:\n92\t\n93\t```\n94\t$ grep -rn 'X-Spaxel-Token' mothership/ | grep -v '_test.go' | grep -v 'cmd/sim/'\n95\t(no output; exit status 1)\n96\t```\n97\t\n98\tThe only remaining references in the tree are the sim's own `Set(...)` calls\n99\tand an e2e test *comment* that asserts this exact fact\n100\t(`mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go:86-88`: *\"spaxel-sim nodes present no\n101\ttoken in their hello message (only the X-Spaxel-Token header, which the\n102\tingestion server does not read)\"*). There is no production code that reads\n103\tit.\n104\t\n105\tThe WS upgrade handler inspects nothing off the incoming request either.\n106\t`mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:455-469`:\n107\t\n108\t```go\n109\tfunc (s *Server) HandleNodeWS(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n110\t    ...\n111\t    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")   // :463 — OUTBOUND response header\n112\t    ...\n113\t    conn, err := s.upgrader.Upgrade(w, r, nil)           // :469 — r passed through, never read for headers\n114\t```\n115\t\n116\tThe sole `Header` reference on this path is `w.Header().Set(...)` on the\n117\t*outbound* response (`:463`); `r` (the request bearing the `X-Spaxel-Token`\n118\theader) is handed straight to `Upgrade` and never inspected. (Every other\n119\t`Header.Get` / `Header.Set` hit under `mothership/internal/` is on an\n120\t*outbound* webhook/notify path or in its tests — `webhook`, `notify`,\n121\t`notifications/ntfy.go`, etc. — none touches the node WS path.)\n122\t\n123\t## Conclusion — the token-supply path is currently DEAD\n124\t\n125\tThe validator reads only `hello.Token`\n126\t(`mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:513`:\n127\t`tokenOK := hello.Token != \"\" && validator(hello.MAC, hello.Token)`), and\n128\tthat field comes only from the JSON body (`message.go:22`). Combining the\n129\tthree facts above:\n130\t\n131\t- The sim supplies its token **only** as the `X-Spaxel-Token` HTTP header\n132\t  (Fact 1);\n133\t- which the mothership **never reads** on the WS path (Fact 3);\n134\t- and the hello body the mothership *does* read has **no `token`** field\n135\t  (Fact 2).\n136\t\n137\tTherefore for every sim node the header is silently dropped, `hello.Token`\n138\tis `\"\"`, and `hello.Token != \"\"` short-circuits `tokenOK` to `false` at\n139\t`server.go:513` **regardless of what token the sim provisioned**. The\n140\tparent bead's \"have the sim supply valid tokens\" option is **currently\n141\tNON-FUNCTIONAL** — the token is supplied to a header nobody reads.\n142\t\n143\t> The token-supply path is dead: header ignored → `hello.Token` empty →\n144\t> `tokenOK` always `false`.\n145\t\n146\tThis isolates the sim/header-side half of `bf-34lwt`. Combined with the\n147\tvalidator-side facts in the sibling note (`bf-5ig3e`: the validator is\n148\talways wired, and acceptance/rejection hinges entirely on `hello.Token`),\n149\tit follows that a sim node is accepted only while the migration window is\n150\topen (the default 24h mask), then `sendReject`-ed with `invalid_token`\n151\t(`server.go:519-528`) once it closes — because the supplied token never\n152\treaches the validator at all.\n153\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5ddf1773-ae76-4160-9d11-f3b1a575141e","uuid":"5add57ad-d0bc-47ca-8b74-2b18bd5fa207","timestamp":"2026-07-07T10:51:41.758Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/spaxel/docs/notes/token-supply-path-dead-confirm.md","content":"# Confirmation: sim supplies its token only as the unread `X-Spaxel-Token` header → token-supply path is DEAD\n\n**Tracking:** bead `bf-29wyl` (second link of the `bf-34lwt` split).\n**Date:** 2026-07-07.\n**Scope:** isolate and re-verify the *sim/header-side* half of the\ntokenless-sim REJECT root cause against current source. This confirms the\nthree statements \"the sim sets the token only as the `X-Spaxel-Token`\nheader\", \"the hello JSON body has no `token` field\", and \"the mothership\nnever reads that header on the WS path\" — i.e. the **token-supply option is\ncurrently dead**. The validator-side half is covered by the sibling note\n[`token-validator-wiring-confirm.md`](./token-validator-wiring-confirm.md)\n(bead `bf-5ig3e`); both roll up into the parent\n[`token-reject-root-cause.md`](./token-reject-root-cause.md) (bead\n`bf-34lwt`). This note re-checks the three header-side facts that chain into\n\"dead path\", so a drift in any of them is detectable on its own.\n\n> **Path convention.** Citations are full repo-relative paths. The `bf-29wyl`\n> task body drops the `mothership/` module prefix, so its `cmd/sim/main.go`\n> is `mothership/cmd/sim/main.go` here.\n>\n> **Two sim copies — both behave identically for this finding.**\n> - `cmd/sim/main.go` — the module `go.work` lists (`use ./cmd/sim`); the\n>   dev/test path. This copy has no `scenario.go`.\n> - `mothership/cmd/sim/` — the copy the **Dockerfile actually builds** (it\n>   does `COPY mothership/ ./` then `go build ./cmd/sim`, so `./cmd/sim`\n>   resolves here).\n\n## Fact 1 — the sim sets its token ONLY as the `X-Spaxel-Token` HTTP header\n\nThe token is attached to the WebSocket *dial* request as a request header in\nall three connect paths, and nowhere else:\n\n- `cmd/sim/main.go:313`: `reqHeader.Set(\"X-Spaxel-Token\", token)` — fed to\n  `websocket.DefaultDialer.DialContext(ctx, u.String(), reqHeader)` (the dial\n  a few lines below).\n- `mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:633`: `headers.Set(\"X-Spaxel-Token\", token)`.\n- `mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:318`: `headers.Set(\"X-Spaxel-Token\", token)`.\n\n`token` here is the provisioned node token (auto-generated to a 64-hex dummy\nif `--token` is empty — `cmd/sim/main.go:120-125`,\n`mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:596-601`). It is supplied to the mothership\nexclusively via this HTTP header on the upgrade request. There is no second\ndelivery channel.\n\n## Fact 2 — the hello JSON body has NO `token` field\n\nThe hello map built and sent as the node's first WS frame omits `token`\nentirely:\n\n- `cmd/sim/main.go:348-359`:\n  ```\n  hello := map[string]interface{}{\n      \"type\":            \"hello\",\n      \"mac\":             macToString(n.MAC),\n      \"firmware_version\": \"sim-1.0.0\",\n      \"capabilities\":    []string{\"csi\", \"ble\", \"tx\", \"rx\"},\n      \"chip\":            \"ESP32-S3\",\n      \"flash_mb\":        16,\n      \"uptime_ms\":       1000,\n      \"pos_x\":           n.Position.X,\n      \"pos_y\":           n.Position.Y,\n      \"pos_z\":           n.Position.Z,\n  }\n  ```\n- `mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:652-665`: keys are\n  `type, mac, firmware_version, capabilities, chip, flash_mb, uptime_ms,\n   wifi_rssi, ip, pos_x, pos_y, pos_z`.\n- `mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:331-342`: keys are\n  `type, mac, firmware_version, capabilities, chip, flash_mb, uptime_ms,\n   wifi_rssi, ip`.\n\nNone of the three contains a `token` key. A targeted grep confirms it — the\nonly `\"token\"` hits in each file are the `--token` *flag definition* (the CLI\nargument), never a JSON body key:\n\n```\n$ grep -n '\"token\"' cmd/sim/main.go mothership/cmd/sim/main.go mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go\ncmd/sim/main.go:54:    flagToken = flag.String(\"token\", \"\", \"Provisioning token (auto-generated if empty)\")\nmothership/cmd/sim/main.go:49:    flagToken = flag.String(\"token\", \"\", \"Provisioning token (auto-generated if empty)\")\nmothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:    (no \"token\" hit at all)\n```\n\n`HelloMessage.Token` carries the JSON tag `json:\"token,omitempty\"`\n(`mothership/internal/ingestion/message.go:22`). Since the body never sets\n`token`, that field deserializes to `\"\"` from either sim binary.\n\n## Fact 3 — the mothership NEVER reads `X-Spaxel-Token` on the WS path\n\nA grep for the literal header name across the entire mothership tree,\nexcluding the sim sources that *set* it and the test files that document the\ncontract, returns **zero** hits:\n\n```\n$ grep -rn 'X-Spaxel-Token' mothership/ | grep -v '_test.go' | grep -v 'cmd/sim/'\n(no output; exit status 1)\n```\n\nThe only remaining references in the tree are the sim's own `Set(...)` calls\nand an e2e test *comment* that asserts this exact fact\n(`mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go:86-88`: *\"spaxel-sim nodes present no\ntoken in their hello message (only the X-Spaxel-Token header, which the\ningestion server does not read)\"*). There is no production code that reads\nit.\n\nThe WS upgrade handler inspects nothing off the incoming request either.\n`mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:455-469`:\n\n```go\nfunc (s *Server) HandleNodeWS(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n    ...\n    w.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")   // :463 — OUTBOUND response header\n    ...\n    conn, err := s.upgrader.Upgrade(w, r, nil)           // :469 — r passed through, never read for headers\n```\n\nThe sole `Header` reference on this path is `w.Header().Set(...)` on the\n*outbound* response (`:463`); `r` (the request bearing the `X-Spaxel-Token`\nheader) is handed straight to `Upgrade` and never inspected. (Every other\n`Header.Get` / `Header.Set` hit under `mothership/internal/` is on an\n*outbound* webhook/notify path or in its tests — `webhook`, `notify`,\n`notifications/ntfy.go`, etc. — none touches the node WS path.)\n\n## Conclusion — the token-supply path is currently DEAD\n\nThe validator reads only `hello.Token`\n(`mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:513`:\n`tokenOK := hello.Token != \"\" && validator(hello.MAC, hello.Token)`), and\nthat field comes only from the JSON body (`message.go:22`). Combining the\nthree facts above:\n\n- The sim supplies its token **only** as the `X-Spaxel-Token` HTTP header\n  (Fact 1);\n- which the mothership **never reads** on the WS path (Fact 3);\n- and the hello body the mothership *does* read has **no `token`** field\n  (Fact 2).\n\nTherefore for every sim node the header is silently dropped, `hello.Token`\nis `\"\"`, and `hello.Token != \"\"` short-circuits `tokenOK` to `false` at\n`server.go:513` **regardless of what token the sim provisioned**. The\nparent bead's \"have the sim supply valid tokens\" option is **currently\nNON-FUNCTIONAL** — the token is supplied to a header nobody reads.\n\n> The token-supply path is dead: header ignored → `hello.Token` empty →\n> `tokenOK` always `false`.\n\nThis isolates the sim/header-side half of `bf-34lwt`. Combined with the\nvalidator-side facts in the sibling note (`bf-5ig3e`: the validator is\nalways wired, and acceptance/rejection hinges entirely on `hello.Token`),\nit follows that a sim node is accepted only while the migration window is\nopen (the default 24h mask), then `sendReject`-ed with `invalid_token`\n(`server.go:519-528`) once it closes — because the supplied token never\nreaches the validator at all.\n","numLines":153,"startLine":1,"totalLines":153}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_0d0662f670af439ba4b84c3b","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t---\n2\tname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\n3\tdescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\n4\tmetadata: \n5\t  node_type: memory\n6\t  type: reference\n7\t  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n8\t---\n9\t\n10\tIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n11\t\n12\t**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n13\t\n14\t**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n15\t1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n16\t2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n17\t3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n18\t   ```python\n19\t   import sqlite3, re\n20\t   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n21\t   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n22\t   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n23\t   con.execute('BEGIN')\n24\t   for rid, ts in rows:\n25\t       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n26\t           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n27\t                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n28\t   con.commit()\n29\t   ```\n30\t   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n31\t4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\n32\tNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n33\t\n34\t**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n35\t```bash\n36\tSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n37\t$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n38\t```\n39\t(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n40\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"5ddf1773-ae76-4160-9d11-f3b1a575141e","uuid":"86050747-8abd-4226-b025-fd9bf4a75d7f","timestamp":"2026-07-07T10:55:07.590Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-spaxel/memory/br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339.md","content":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments.\n\n**Update (2026-07-06):** the *global* break is fixed — 12269 of ~12271 `worker_sessions` rows are now valid RFC3339 and beads do close. But the claim-write bug **persists for new dispatches**: a fresh claim still writes `claimed_at` as naive `CURRENT_TIMESTAMP` (`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS`, length 19), so expect ~1–2 bad rows per new dispatch — the targeted repair above is **recurring**, not one-time. Confirmed again on bf-18yn (two length-19 rows from today's dispatch blocked the close; converting them unblocked it). The `sqlite3` binary IS available via Nix at `/nix/store/*-sqlite-3.51.2-bin/bin/sqlite3` (not on PATH) — a one-liner repair works:\n```bash\nSQLITE=$(ls -d /nix/store/*-sqlite-*-bin/bin/sqlite3 | head -1)\n$SQLITE .beads/beads.db \"UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S+00:00',claimed_at) WHERE claimed_at LIKE '____-__-__ __:__:__';\"\n```\n(`python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"` still works as a fallback if no Nix sqlite3 is present.)\n","numLines":40,"startLine":1,"totalLines":40}}}
